label: Drug discovery, protein & molecular design

node.label: Drug discovery, protein & molecular design
node.definition: Drug, protein, and molecular-design systems predict properties and generate structures under chemical, biological, geometric, and experimental constraints.
node.why_it_matters: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
examples: workflows combining Drug discovery, protein & molecular design with Graph transformers
workflows combining Drug discovery, protein & molecular design with Diffusion & score-based models
workflows combining Drug discovery, protein & molecular design with Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Graph transformers, Diffusion & score-based models.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Drug discovery, protein & molecular design has an `applies` dependency on Graph transformers with `common` strength.
Drug discovery, protein & molecular design integrates Diffusion & score-based models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Drug discovery, protein & molecular design has an `applies` dependency on Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Drug discovery, protein & molecular design has an `applies` dependency on Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins with `common` strength.
Drug discovery, protein & molecular design integrates Graph neural networks as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Drug discovery, protein & molecular design integrates Variational autoencoders as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Drug discovery, protein & molecular design has an `applies` dependency on Neural fields & radiance-field models with `optional` strength.
